x4freetext: Laboratory (TUNL) using a 10 MV FN Tandem Van de
neutrons between 0.5 and 15 MeV were produced
The FPY measurements were conducted with neutron
fluxes ranging from 2*10**6 to 5*10**7 n s-1cm-2 at 5
cm from the neutron production cell.
ionization chambers(DFCs) were dedicated to one of
the three fissionable isotopes studied in this work:
detectors. Each detector is housed within a custom
lead shield in the TUNL low-background counting
A single DFC contains two thin (10-100 microg cm-2)
reference foils and a thick (~200 mg cm-2) actinide
activation target. The activation target is
contained in the center of the chamber while the thin
reference foils are 2.67 mm up- and down-stream from
the activation target. The thick activation target is
composed of the same actinide material as the thin
reference foils in the adjacent chambers.
Fission yields were determined by activation
method using characteristic gamma rays of fission
Photo-peak area 0.2-1% and Fission chamber counts
Neutron flux at the activation foil.
